The patient presents with right upper quadrant pain, fever, and leukocytosis — classic signs of acute cholecystitis. The absence of significant elevation in bilirubin, along with normal liver enzyme patterns, suggests no major biliary obstruction. Cholescintigraphy showing patency of all biliary structures rules out downstream obstruction like common bile duct stones. Given the constellation of findings—right upper quadrant pain, fever, leukocytosis, and normal imaging—the most likely cause is obstruction of the cystic duct leading to cholecystitis.
